worthClicking.author: @SuhailKakar
worthClicking.why: Brutally honest first take on Opus 4.7: 68 minutes and millions of tokens later, nothing worked — a useful data point on autonomous agent reliability for anyone building on it.

worthClicking.author: @ClaudeDevs
worthClicking.why: Official confirmation that rate limits on Opus 4.7 were misconfigured at launch and have now been reset — operational signal for anyone hitting limits.

worthClicking.author: @alexalbert__
worthClicking.why: Insider breakdown of Opus 4.7's actual differentiators: predictable effort levels, xhigh token control, no image downscaling, and better UI taste — the real feature list.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison found a local 21GB Qwen model beating Opus 4.7 on his benchmark — raises real questions about the value proposition of frontier pricing.

worthClicking.author: @Polymarket
worthClicking.why: White House preparing to give US agencies access to Claude Mythos — a major government distribution move that changes Anthropic's strategic position.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: OpenAI's Codex update drops background computer use on macOS one hour after Opus 4.7 — the competitive timing and feature set matter for anyone watching the agent platform race.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: OpenAI's Greg Brockman on Codex's cross-tool retrieval across Slack, Docs, and Notion feeling like magic — early signal of what enterprise agent distribution looks like.

worthClicking.author: @xw33bttv
worthClicking.why: Anthropic requiring government ID for Claude Code is drawing strong consumer backlash — a product decision that could hurt indie dev adoption at the exact moment of a major launch.

worthClicking.author: @fuckgrowth
worthClicking.why: Short thread arguing we're entering a distribution supercycle — directly relevant to how indie devs should think about go-to-market right now.

worthClicking.author: @rxhit05
worthClicking.why: Concrete checklist of why vibe-coded SaaS fails post-launch: security and ops gaps that any indie dev shipping fast should audit against.

voices.why: Anthropic insider with specific, useful takes on model capabilities — not hype, actual feature details.
Rigorous empirical benchmarking of AI models; cuts through launch hype with real comparisons.
Power user perspective on Claude degradation — surfaces product reliability issues that matter for builders.
Greg Brockman's ground-level observations on Codex agent behavior are rare signal from inside OpenAI.
Thinking seriously about distribution strategy in the AI era — relevant for indie dev go-to-market.

examples: Elon/politics engagement bait with no text context
crypto stock shilling and financial noise
